title:
Module 3
SFW Image Creation
subtitle: Learn How to Generate SFW Images from Reference Images
output_filename: Module 3 - SFW Image Creation/Module 3 - SFW Image Creation.pdf

blocks.content: The reference image workflow is the backbone of your content pipeline. Instead of writing prompts from scratch and hoping for good results, you find real photos that have the exact pose, lighting, and setting you want — then you recreate them with your AI model. This is how you produce content that looks like a real photoshoot, not random AI generations.

Nano Banana 2 is your main tool for all SFW image generation. The workflow is simple: find a reference image on Pinterest, generate a JSON prompt from it, then feed that JSON prompt into Nano Banana 2 along with your base model image. The output matches the reference image's pose and lighting — but with your AI model's face and body.

items: Find a reference image on Pinterest with the pose, lighting, and setting you want
Generate a JSON prompt from that reference image
Feed the JSON prompt into Nano Banana 2 along with your base model image
Nano Banana 2 generates an image that copies the pose and lighting from the reference — but with your AI model
Generate at least 3 outputs and compare — pick the one that best matches your model's face
If the face drifts, regenerate — do not settle for inconsistent results
Pinterest is your main source — search for poses, outfits, and settings you want to recreate
Look for images with clear lighting and simple backgrounds — these produce the best results
Save references into categories (casual, glam, gym, selfie, etc.) so you always have ideas ready
The better the reference image, the better your output — do not use blurry or cluttered photos
Using blurry or low-quality reference images from Pinterest — find clean, well-lit photos
Picking reference poses that are too complex — start with simple, natural poses
Ignoring face drift on 'almost good enough' images — never post a drifted face
Only generating one version — always generate at least 3 and pick the best
Not saving your working JSON prompts — keep a library of prompts that produce good results

sections.header: Lesson 2: Prompt Engineering & Editing AI Images

left.content: Why JSON Prompts Beat Traditional Prompts

Your prompt is everything. JSON prompts are the best way to prompt because they carry far more data about posing, lighting, camera settings, and environment than a traditional text prompt ever could. A JSON prompt specifies exact depth of field, shadow behavior, color temperature, and composition — details that get lost in a one-line text prompt. The AI gets structured instructions for every aspect of the image instead of guessing.

Traditional prompt:
'Woman walking on city street, blue gingham outfit, sunny day, fashion photography, photorealistic'

This tells the AI nothing about pose, lighting direction, camera angle, depth of field, color grading, or composition. You get random results every time.

JSON prompt advantage:
→ Composition: exact framing, crop, camera angle, depth of field
→ Subject: specific wardrobe details, expression, gesture
→ Environment: location elements, architectural details
→ Lighting: source, direction, color temperature, shadow behavior
→ Technical: focal length, aperture, ISO, sharpness
→ Artistic: aesthetic, mood, stylistic influences, texture rendering

Every detail is specified — nothing is left to chance.

items: Find your reference image on Pinterest
Feed it into an AI and ask it to generate a JSON prompt describing everything about the image
The AI breaks down pose, lighting, environment, camera settings, and style into structured JSON
Feed that JSON prompt into Nano Banana 2 along with your base model image
The output matches the reference's pose and lighting — but with your AI model

right.label: Example JSON Prompt:

blocks.title: Pro Tip
blocks.content: Save every JSON prompt that produces good results. Build a library organized by scene type (street, cafe, gym, beach, home, etc.). When you need new content, grab a working JSON prompt, swap out the wardrobe and environment details, and generate. This is how you produce dozens of images efficiently without starting from scratch.

Raw AI outputs are rarely perfect. Even the best generations need minor editing before they are ready to post. This lesson covers what to look for, how to fix common issues, and the critical trick that makes AI images pass as real photos. Skipping the editing step is how pages get exposed as AI.

items: Extra or distorted fingers/hands — the most common AI artifact
Asymmetric facial features — one eye larger or different height
Text or watermark artifacts in background — AI loves generating fake text
Unnatural skin smoothing or plastic shine — too perfect is too fake
Weird jewelry, accessories, or clothing seams that break reality
Background inconsistencies — impossible geometry, blending errors, floating objects
Photopea (photopea.com) — Free browser-based Photoshop alternative. Handles everything you need
GIMP — Free desktop app for advanced editing
Canva — Basic cropping, filters, and text overlay
Snapseed — Mobile editing for quick adjustments
For most editing tasks, Photopea is all you need

left.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
left.content: Before/after examples of common AI artifacts being fixed: distorted hand corrected, background text removed, skin smoothing reduced, asymmetric eyes fixed

items: Step 1: Inspect hands and fingers first (most common issue)
Step 2: Check face symmetry and eye alignment
Step 3: Scan background for text, artifacts, or impossible geometry
Step 4: Adjust color grading for natural skin tones
Step 5: Add approximately 2% grain/noise for realism (critical step)
Step 6: Crop or reframe for platform-specific aspect ratios
Step 7: Export at appropriate resolution

right.content: This is the single most important editing technique in this entire course. Raw AI images look too clean, too smooth, too perfect. Real photos from phones have subtle grain and noise. Adding approximately 2% grain in your editing tool makes AI images pass as real.

items: In Photopea: Filter → Noise → Add Noise → set to 2-3%, Gaussian, Monochromatic
This simulates camera sensor noise that exists in every real photo
Without grain, experienced eyes can spot AI images immediately
The difference is subtle but it is the difference between 'this looks real' and 'this looks AI'
Apply grain as the very last step before export

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Side by side comparison: Raw AI output (too smooth, no grain, plastic-looking) vs Edited output (grain added, color corrected, natural-looking). Label the differences clearly.
Nano Banana 2 Edit Feature — Fixing Specific Areas

When a generated image is almost perfect but has one bad area (a distorted hand, a weird background element), use the edit feature in Nano Banana 2 to fix just that area without regenerating the entire image.

items: Use the edit feature in Nano Banana 2 to select the problem area
Write a prompt describing what should be in that area
Nano Banana 2 regenerates only the selected region while keeping everything else
This saves images that would otherwise be rejected entirely
Use it for: hands, background artifacts, clothing seams, jewelry errors

blocks.title: Rule
blocks.content: Do not over-edit. The goal is to fix obvious AI artifacts and add grain, not to create a heavily processed image. Over-edited photos look fake in a different way. Fix the problems, add grain, and stop. Natural-looking beats polished-looking every time.

Each platform has different content guidelines. What works on Reddit will get you banned on Instagram. Understanding what is 'safe' per platform prevents account strikes and keeps your content flowing without interruption. One wrong post can shadowban your account for weeks. Know the rules before you post.

items: No nudity, implied or explicit — zero tolerance
No underwear as primary clothing (bras/panties as the outfit)
Cleavage must be modest and natural — no extreme push-up
Swimwear is allowed in context (beach, pool settings only)
No sexual poses or suggestive hand placement
No sexual captions or innuendo in text
AI-generated content is increasingly flagged — realism is critical
Similar rules to Instagram, often stricter enforcement
Carousels and photo content are the safest format
Avoid anything that could be flagged as 'adult content'
When in doubt, keep it casual and fully clothed
TikTok's moderation is faster and harsher than Instagram's
One content strike can shadow your account for weeks
Linked to Instagram — violations can affect your IG account
Text-based posts are safest for Threads
Optional photo attachments should be SFW casual only
No explicit images, no suggestive poses in attached photos
Keep it conversational — Threads is for personality, not photos
Rules vary dramatically by subreddit — always read before posting
SFW subreddits still require tasteful, appropriate content
Some SFW subs allow swimwear, others do not
NSFW subreddits have their own specific content rules
Mark your profile as NSFW in settings before any NSFW posting
Moderators are human and strict — one violation can get you permanently banned from a subreddit
More lenient than Instagram overall
SFW content actually performs better for discovery and growth
Mark sensitive content appropriately in media settings
Keep main feed clean — use the sensitive content flag for anything borderline
Explicit content is allowed but must be properly marked

right.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
right.content: Platform safety scale: horizontal gradient from 'Strictest' to 'Most Flexible'. Left: TikTok → Instagram → Threads → Twitter/X → Reddit. Show what's allowed at each level with checkmark and X icons for content types.

items: Is the outfit something you would see at a mall? → Safe for Instagram/TikTok
Is there visible underwear as the main outfit? → Not safe for Instagram
Would this photo make you uncomfortable if your boss saw it? → Borderline — test with a trial post first
Could this be interpreted as sexual? → Do not post on Instagram or TikTok

blocks.title: Golden Rule
blocks.content: When in doubt, keep it safe. One content strike on Instagram can shadow your account for weeks. It is always better to post something slightly too modest than to risk a ban. You can always push boundaries gradually once trust is established — you cannot undo a strike.

summary_points: Always generate new images using your base model as a reference in Nano Banana 2
Write detailed prompts with setting, lighting, outfit, and camera angle — save templates that produce good results
Use Photopea (free) to export Instagram carousels — this helps avoid the AI filter on Instagram
Always add approximately 2% grain/noise as the final editing step — this is what makes AI images pass as real photos
Use the edit feature in Nano Banana 2 to fix specific problem areas without regenerating the entire image
Know each platform's content rules — TikTok and Instagram are strictest, Reddit is most flexible
When in doubt, keep it safe — a content strike can derail weeks of progress
